scope: Project Work task metadata PATCH concurrent duplicate/stale-version behavior.
expected: Two concurrent duplicate task PATCH requests using the same clientUpdatedAt should serialize through optimistic concurrency: exactly one request succeeds, the loser receives task_version_conflict, final task/planning readback reflects the winner once, and side effects are single-use.
actual: Pass: concurrent duplicate task PATCH returned one 200 and one 409 task_version_conflict; task detail readback showed the winner title, planning read-model planVersion incremented once, task detail showed one system activity, and audit events contained exactly one task.updated event for the target task.
preCoverageGap: The full-evaluation write-flow matrix had project-task create/archive and task-status coverage, but no fresh duplicate/concurrent evidence for task metadata PATCH with clientUpdatedAt optimistic concurrency.
codeChanges: apps/api/src/projectWorkRoutes.db.test.ts adds a DB/API regression covering concurrent duplicate PATCH, winner/loser responses, task readback, planning read-model version bump, system activity count, and audit count.
No production code change was needed for this atom; existing task update locking and clientUpdatedAt version checks already produced the expected 200/409 single-use behavior.

verification.command: DATABASE_URL=<local-test-db> node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run --config vitest.db.config.ts apps/api/src/projectWorkRoutes.db.test.ts -t "keeps concurrent duplicate task PATCH updates"
verification.notes: Vitest emitted the existing duplicate assignment.delete warning in packages/domain/src/planning/commandReducer.ts, unrelated to this slice.

coverageImpact: Adds fresh DB/API evidence for Project Work task PATCH duplicate/concurrent optimistic-concurrency behavior. Broader RISK-WRITE-FLOW-RACE-IDEMPOTENCY remains open for other write surfaces such as call participant-state, meeting create duplicate submit, control-surface publish/rollback, retrospective close, and comment idempotency policy.
